text: To clarify
To rephrase
To catch
Confusing
To make sense
Vague
To hesitate
Filler
To recap
To bear with
To hold on
Straightforward
Sorry, could you say that again?
What do you mean by that?
Let me think for a second.
So, just to recap, we meet at three?
Hi Greg. You said there is a change to our plan for Saturday?
Yes! We are meeting at the new place on Hazelwood Avenue, behind the old cinema, at half past six.
Sorry, I did not catch the street. Could you say that again?
Of course. Hazelwood Avenue, the street right behind the old cinema.
Got it. And what do you mean by half past six? Six thirty, right?
Exactly, six thirty. Bear with me, I know I explained it fast.
No problem. Hold on -- let me just recap so I do not get it wrong.
Please do. I would rather repeat it now than wait for you later!
So, Hazelwood Avenue, behind the cinema, at six thirty on Saturday. Does that make sense?
That is exactly right. See you there!
What do you mean by that, exactly?
Could you rephrase that in simpler words?
So, just to recap, is that correct?

Here is something I wish someone had told me years ago: it is completely normal not to understand every word, even in your own language. When a person speaks too fast and you do not catch something, you do not have to pretend. Just ask. You can say, Sorry, could you say that again? Or, if a word is confusing, ask them to rephrase it in simpler English. If you need a moment to think, use a filler like well or let me see, and hold on for a second. And before you finish, do a quick recap: repeat the main points back to check. Asking people to clarify is not a weakness. It is the most confident thing a good communicator does.

Hi Ana Paula, it is Sarah! I have to tell you what happened on the phone this morning. I called the dentist, and the receptionist gave me the new address so fast that I did not catch the street number at all. The old me would have just said okay and hoped for the best. But this time I said, Sorry, could you clarify that? and she happily repeated it. One part was still vague, so I asked her to rephrase it, and then, just to recap, I read the whole address back to her. It took thirty extra seconds, and now I actually know where to go! Honestly, do not hesitate to ask people to slow down. It works every time. Okay, call me back when you can. Bye!

Last week I called a clinic to book a checkup. The receptionist spoke fast, and I did not catch the time. I asked her to clarify and to rephrase it in simpler words. I used a filler to hold on and think for a second. To recap, I repeated the address back to check I understood.

Now your explanation makes sense to me.

So, just to recap, we meet at three, right?
Sorry, could you say that again?
